---
title: "Code Repository"
description: "GitHub repositories and reproducible notebooks behind every experiment on this site."
toc: true
---

Every data-driven claim on this site is backed by code that can be run and checked.
Reproducibility is the first principle here: a result isn't a finding until someone
else can reproduce it.

## This website
The full source for davidmaguire.ai is public and built with Quarto:
[github.com/davidolivermaguire-ai/davidmaguire.ai](https://github.com/davidolivermaguire-ai/davidmaguire.ai)

## Research code
Each [Quant Research Lab](quant-lab/index.qmd) experiment links to its own
reproducible script or notebook. For example, the Monte-Carlo behind
[How Backtest Selection Inflates the Sharpe Ratio](quant-lab/backtest-overfitting/index.qmd)
lives in `code/backtest_overfitting.py` in the repository above.

## Principles
- **Reproducibility first** — code is published alongside the result it produces.
- **Versioned** — everything is tracked in Git, so results can be traced to the exact code that generated them.
- **Honest** — the code shows the full method, including the parts that didn't work.
